purpose: Per-parser-family AST-shape contract for the SystemVerilog grammar. The generated SV parser is NOT in the default `cargo test --features generated_parsers` build; it is produced on-demand by `sv_stimuli_quality_gate` (and similar) into `rust/target/<gate>/work/systemverilog_parser.rs` and discarded. The cfg `has_generated_systemverilog_parser` therefore stays off in default builds and the matching unit test compiles out. When the parser is present (gate run or `PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_PATH` override), the test activates and the contract is enforced.
doctrine: Calibrated 2026-05-04 against a freshly-generated parser (parser release 1.0.1, contract 1.0.1). On first run with the parser available, this manifest is expected to match â€” `current_content_kind: "json_object"` was confirmed by running `parseability_probe --parse-dump-ast-pretty systemverilog /tmp/sv_calibration/minimal_module.sv` after the SV-Slice-1 grammar fix that moved the dangling `systemverilog_file` annotation onto the rule it was supposed to attach to. Future drift fails the regression-lock and surfaces the change to the slice that introduced it.
calibration_history: 2026-05-05 SV-Slice-12 (batch): UDP declaration family typed (4 rules; mirrors module/interface/program structural pattern with one wrinkle). (a) `udp_ansi_declaration` typed `{attributes, name, ports}` (drops kw_primitive, parens, semi). (b) `udp_nonansi_declaration` same field names (only ports source rule differs). (c) `udp_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi/wildcard). (d) `udp_declaration_sv_2023` mirror with positional shift in wildcard. **Special: nonansi branch has `udp_port_declaration udp_port_declaration*` mini-mixed-array** (a required first + repeat) â€” used `port_decls: {first: $2, rest: $3}` workaround (same idiom as attribute_instance from slice 6). Annotation count: 79 (was 67, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-11 (batch): program-header sub-tree typed (mirror of module/interface header pattern). (a) `program_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (b) `program_nonansi_header` same field names (only ports source rule differs â€” list_of_ports vs (list_of_port_declarations)?). For `program p; endprogram\n`: header.name = 'p' (clean string from slice 8), all 6 named fields populated. Annotation count: 67 (was 65, +2). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-10 (batch): class + package + program declarations typed. (a) `class_declaration_sv_2017` â€” single sequence typed `{virtual, lifetime, name, parameters, extends, implements, items, end_label}`. (b) `class_declaration_sv_2023` â€” same shape with `final_specifier` instead of `lifetime` (semantically distinct field per LRM 2023). (c) `package_declaration` â€” single sequence typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, timeunits, items, end_label}` (annotation registered; runtime parse on bare `package p; endpackage` input fails at position 0 â€” likely pre-existing issue, not introduced by this slice; module/interface/program tests still pass with same parser). (d) `program_declaration_sv_2017` â€” 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). NOTE: program puts nonansi BEFORE ansi in the rule definition (different from module/interface) â€” kind labels still discriminate correctly. (e) `program_declaration_sv_2023` â€” same 5 kinds with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `program p; endprogram\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind = 'ansi'. Annotation count: 65 (was 53, +12). Same accept set across all annotated rules.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-9 (batch): interface declarations typed â€” full mirror of the module pattern. (a) `interface_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}` (no keyword field â€” only one keyword exists for interfaces). (b) `interface_nonansi_header` same field names. (c) `interface_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). (d) `interface_declaration_sv_2023` same kind set with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `interface bus; endinterface\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind='ansi', header.name='bus' (clean string, inherited from slice 8 identifier typing). Annotation count: 53 (was 41, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-8 (batch): four identifier-leaf rules typed (`-> $2` to drop trivia + surface the regex-captured identifier name as a clean string). (a) `simple_identifier := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*/ -> $2`. (b) `escaped_identifier := trivia /\\[!-~]+/ -> $2`. (c) `non_keyword_identifier := !reserved_non_keyword_identifier identifier -> $2`. (d) `simple_identifier_no_scope := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*(?![ \t\r\n]*::)/ -> $2`. **Massive propagation effect**: these are leaf rules consumed by `identifier`, `declaration_identifier`, `module_identifier`, `class_identifier`, `package_identifier`, `interface_identifier`, etc. â€” each of which is a transparent passthrough alias. After this slice, ALL identifier-typed fields (anywhere in any rule that ultimately resolves to an identifier) surface as clean JSON strings instead of the raw envelope chain. For `module m; endmodule\n`: `header.name` was `[[], [[], "m"]]` (raw envelope) â†’ now `"m"` (clean string). Annotation count: 41 (was 37, +4). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-7 (batch): four rules typed in one slice. (a) `module_keyword` per-branch typed (`{kind: "module"}` / `{kind: "macromodule"}`). (b) `lifetime` per-branch typed (`{kind: "static"}` / `{kind: "automatic"}`). (c) `module_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, keyword, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (d) `module_nonansi_header` typed with same field names (only `ports` source differs â€” `list_of_ports` vs `(list_of_port_declarations)?`). For `module m; endmodule\n`: the previously-raw `header:` field of the ansi-kind module_declaration_sv_2017 now resolves to a typed object with `keyword: {kind: "module"}` (via slice 7a) and named optional/quantified fields. **Four layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end**: source_text_item.kind â†’ description.kind â†’ module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind â†’ module_<form>_header (with sub-typed keyword). Annotation count: 37 (was 31, +6: 2 module_keyword, 2 lifetime, 1 module_ansi_header, 1 module_nonansi_header). Same accept set across all four slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-6 (batch): three rules typed in one slice. (a) `attribute_instance` annotated `{first: $2, rest: $3}` â€” drops attr_open/close delimiters, exposes first attr_spec + trailing reps. (b) `module_declaration_sv_2017` per-branch typed (5 branches: ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi) with kind discriminator and structured fields (header/timeunits/items/end_label, plus attributes/keyword/lifetime/name on the wildcard branch). (c) `module_declaration_sv_2023` same per-branch typing as sv_2017 (kind set is identical; only the wildcard branch's positional indices differ â€” sv_2023 uses `dot star` (2 tokens) vs sv_2017's `dot_star` (1 token), shifting timeunits from $9â†’$10, items from $10â†’$11, end_label from $12â†’$13). Three layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end: source_text_item.kind â†’ description.kind â†’ module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind. For `module m; endmodule\n` (sv_2017 profile): source_text[0].body.body now resolves to `{kind: "ansi", header: <module_ansi_header envelope>, timeunits: [], items: [], end_label: []}`. Annotation count: 31 (was 20). NOTE: `comment_only_source_region` typing was attempted in this batch but BLOCKED by task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug); annotation didn't register and was reverted. Same accept set across all three slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-5: `compiler_directive` transparent passthrough of directive text (`-> $2`). For an input with `` `define FOO bar `` followed by `module m; endmodule\n`, source_text now has 2 items: item[0] is `{kind: "compiler_directive", body: "`define FOO bar"}` (body is now a clean string instead of nested envelope), item[1] is the typed module description. Drops the `trivia` prefix slot from the matched sequence; emits just the captured directive text. Annotation count: 20 (was 19). Same accept set; only the compiler_directive shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-4: `description` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated. Branches 1-5 + 8 (single-element): `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}`. Branches 6-7 (multi-element `attribute_instance* X`): `{kind: "<branch_name>", attributes: $1, body: $2}` â€” preserves attribute_instance* prefix as a separate `attributes` field. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0].body is now `{"kind": "module_declaration", "body": <envelope>}` (was raw envelope). Two layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end (source_text_item.kind â†’ description.kind). Annotation count: 19 (was 11). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-3: `source_text_item` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated with `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}` â€” kinds: description, local_parameter_declaration, parameter_declaration, package_import_declaration, timeunits_declaration, compiler_directive, comment_only_source_region, semi (the last carries no body since it's just `;`). Pre-fix each source_text_item carried the matched-branch shape directly without a discriminator; post-fix consumers can dispatch on `item["kind"]`. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0] is now `{"kind": "description", "body": <description envelope>}`. Annotation count: 11 (was 3). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-2: `source_text := source_text_item*` annotated `-> [$1**]`. Pre-fix `source_text` field of `systemverilog_file` was a nested Quantified envelope; post-fix it's a flat array of `source_text_item` shapes. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text now has length 1 (the single module_declaration's source_text_item envelope) instead of the Quantified wrapper. Annotation count: 3 (was 2). Same accept set; only the source_text array shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-1: dangling annotation `-> {type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: $2}` at grammars/systemverilog.ebnf line 195 was moved onto its target rule `systemverilog_file` (line 184). Pre-fix the annotation was lost (separated from its rule by a blank line + the `sv_multi_entry_root` helper definition + another blank line) and the parser registered 0 annotations effective on systemverilog_file. Post-fix the parser registers 2 annotations (systemverilog_file + systemverilog_parseable_file) and the AST root for `module m; endmodule\n` is the typed `{type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: ...}` JSON object documented below.

samples.name: minimal_module
samples.drift_tracked_in: SV-Slice-12 batch (parser release 1.0.12 / contract 1.0.12) â€” UDP declaration family typed. minimal_module sample is unaffected. See docs/contracts/PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md Â§ "Release 1.0.12 / Contract 1.0.12 Highlights".
